# Rash

> Rash is a declarative shell scripting language using Ansible-like YAML syntax, compiled to a single Rust binary. Designed for container entrypoints, IoT devices, and local scripting with zero dependencies.


## What is Rash

Rash provides:
- A **simple syntax** to maintain low complexity
- One static binary to be **container oriented**
- A **declarative** syntax to be idempotent
- **Clear output** to log properly
- **Security** by design
- **Speed and efficiency**
- **Modular** design
- Support of [MiniJinja](https://docs.rs/minijinja/latest/minijinja/syntax/index.html) **templates**


## Installation

```bash
# Download latest binary (Linux/macOS)
curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/rash-sh/rash/releases/latest \
    | grep browser_download_url \
    | grep -v sha256 \
    | grep $(uname -m) \
    | grep $(uname | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') \
    | grep -v musl \
    | cut -d '"' -f 4 \
    | xargs curl -s -L \
    | sudo tar xvz -C /usr/local/bin
```


## Quick Start

Create an `entrypoint.rh` file:

```yaml
- name: Ensure directory exists
  file:
    path: /app/data
    state: directory

- name: Copy configuration
  copy:
    content: "{{ env.APP_CONFIG }}"
    dest: /app/config.yml

- name: Run application
  command:
    cmd: /app/bin/start
```


## Modules

Rash modules are idempotent operations like Ansible.

- alternatives
- apk
- apt
- apt_repository
- archive
- assemble
- assert
- async_poll
- async_status
- at
- authorized_key
- blkdiscard
- block
- cargo
- chroot
- command
- composer
- consul_kv
- copy
- cron
- dconf
- debconf
- debootstrap
- debug
- dmsetup
- dnf
- docker_compose
- docker_config
- docker_container
- docker_exec
- docker_image
- docker_prune
- docker_volume
- expect
- fail
- fail2ban
- file
- filesystem
- find
- firewalld
- flatpak
- gem
- get_url
- git
- gpg_key
- group
- grub
- helm
- helm_info
- hostname
- include
- ini_file
- initramfs
- interfaces_file
- iptables
- java_keystore
- jenkins_job
- json_file
- kernel_blacklist
- known_hosts
- kubectl
- lbu
- lineinfile
- locale
- logrotate
- lvg
- lvol
- lxd_container
- make
- mdadm
- meta
- modprobe
- mongodb_db
- mongodb_user
- mount
- mysql_db
- netplan
- nftables
- nmcli
- npm
- openssl_certificate
- openssl_csr
- openssl_privatekey
- opkg
- package
- pacman
- pam_limits
- parted
- pause
- pids
- ping
- pip
- postgresql_db
- rabbitmq_user
- reboot
- redis
- runit
- script
- seboolean
- selinux
- service
- set_vars
- setup
- sgdisk
- slurp
- ssh_config
- stat
- synchronize
- sysctl
- systemd
- tempfile
- template
- timezone
- trace
- ufw
- unarchive
- uri
- user
- vault
- vdo
- wait_for
- wipefs
- xattr
- xml
- yum_repository
- zfs
- zpool
- zypper

See https://rash-sh.github.io/docs/rash/latest/modules.html for full documentation.


## Lookups

Lookups allow fetching data from external sources.

- file
- find
- password
- passwordstore
- pipe
- vault

See https://rash-sh.github.io/docs/rash/latest/lookups.html for full documentation.


## Built-in Variables

- `rash.path` - Path to the current script
- `rash.dir` - Directory of the current script
- `rash.cwd` - Current working directory
- `rash.arch` - System architecture
- `rash.check_mode` - Boolean indicating check mode
- `env.VAR_NAME` - Access environment variables

See https://rash-sh.github.io/docs/rash/latest/builtins.html for full documentation.


## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/rash-sh/rash
- Documentation: https://rash-sh.github.io/docs/rash/latest/
